questions.prompt: Given the function f(x) = 3x² - 2x + 5, what is f(-2)?
questions.explanation: To evaluate f(-2), substitute x = -2 into the function: f(-2) = 3(-2)² - 2(-2) + 5 = 3(4) + 4 + 5 = 12 + 4 + 5 = 21. This demonstrates the substitution method: replace every instance of the variable with the given value, then follow order of operations to simplify.
